title: Quiz: Rydberg Blockade Mechanism
description: Test your understanding of Rydberg blockade physics, collective dynamics, and gate implementations

questions.question: What is the physical mechanism behind Rydberg blockade?

options.text: Atoms physically collide and scatter when both are excited
The strong Rydberg-Rydberg interaction shifts the doubly-excited state out of resonance
The laser power is insufficient to excite two atoms
Pauli exclusion prevents two atoms from occupying the same Rydberg state

questions.explanation: Rydberg blockade occurs because the van der Waals interaction V(R) = C6/R^6 between two Rydberg atoms shifts the doubly-excited state |rr> out of resonance with the excitation laser. When V >> Omega (the Rabi frequency), the transition to |rr> is effectively forbidden.

questions.question: How does the blockade radius R_b scale with the Rabi frequency Omega?
questions.explanation: The blockade radius is R_b = (C6/hbar*Omega)^(1/6). This means R_b scales as Omega^(-1/6). Increasing the Rabi frequency decreases the blockade radius because faster oscillations can overcome weaker interactions.

questions.question: For two atoms within the blockade radius, what is the collective Rabi frequency compared to the single-atom Rabi frequency?
questions.explanation: The collective (symmetric) state |+> = (|gr> + |rg>)/sqrt(2) couples to |gg> with an enhanced Rabi frequency of sqrt(2)*Omega. This is because both atoms contribute coherently to the coupling. For N atoms, the enhancement is sqrt(N).

questions.question: In the standard Rydberg CZ gate protocol, what happens when both atoms start in state |11>?

options.text: Both atoms are excited to Rydberg simultaneously
The first atom blocks the second, resulting in a pi phase on |11>
Neither atom can be excited due to mutual blockade

questions.explanation: In the CZ protocol: (1) First atom does pi pulse to Rydberg, (2) Second atom attempts 2pi pulse but is blocked, (3) First atom returns from Rydberg. The |11> state acquires a pi phase because the blocked 2pi rotation behaves differently than an unblocked one.

questions.question: How does the blockade error scale with the ratio R/R_b (distance to blockade radius)?
questions.explanation: The blockade error scales as (Omega/V)^2 = (R/R_b)^12 because V ~ 1/R^6 and error ~ 1/V^2 ~ R^12. This very steep scaling means staying well within the blockade radius is crucial for high-fidelity gates.

questions.question: What is 'anti-blockade' or 'facilitation' in Rydberg physics?

options.text: A condition where blockade is especially strong
When the laser detuning matches the interaction energy, enhancing double excitation
The opposite of blockade - atoms repel each other
A technique to increase the blockade radius

questions.explanation: Anti-blockade (facilitation) occurs when the laser is detuned by exactly the interaction energy: delta = V(R). In this case, single-atom excitation is suppressed (off-resonant) but two-atom excitation is resonant, enhancing rather than blocking the double excitation.

questions.question: In a perfectly blockaded system of N atoms, a 2pi pulse returns all population to the ground state regardless of N.
questions.explanation: True. In perfect blockade, only the symmetric single-excitation state |W> couples to |G> (all ground). The system undergoes Rabi oscillations between |G> and |W> at frequency sqrt(N)*Omega. A 2pi pulse at this collective frequency returns all population to |G>, regardless of N.

questions.question: Which factors limit the fidelity of Rydberg blockade gates? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Finite blockade strength causing residual |rr> population
Spontaneous emission from the Rydberg state
Perfect atom localization within the trap

questions.explanation: Gate fidelity is limited by (a) finite blockade causing leakage to |rr>, (b) spontaneous emission during the gate, and (c) Doppler shifts from thermal motion. Perfect localization (d) would actually HELP fidelity, not limit it - it's the imperfect localization that causes errors through distance fluctuations.
